rxn_class_summary: Cp2Fe-mediated electrochemical regioselective ortho-(4+2) cyclization and ipso-(3+2) spirocyclization of benzylic malonates with alkenes and alkynes to give tetrahydronaphthalenes and spirocarbocycles
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of Cp2Fe to Cp2Fe+ mediator, which oxidizes the deprotonated malonate anion via single-electron transfer to generate a malonyl C-radical; cathode reduces MeOH to MeO- and H2

key_intermediates.name: alkyl radical adduct II (from addition to alkene)
vinyl radical adduct III (from addition to alkyne)
cyclohexadienyl radical IV (after ortho-(4+2) cyclization onto aryl)
spirocyclic cyclohexadienyl radical V (after ipso-(3+2) cyclization)
spirocyclic cation VI (after SET oxidation of V)
dimethoxy spirocyclic intermediate VII (MeO- trapping of VI)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes Cp2Fe to Cp2Fe+ while cathode reduces MeOH to MeO- + H2; MeO- deprotonates the benzylic malonate, and Cp2Fe+ oxidizes the anion by SET to a malonyl radical that adds to alkene/alkyne. The resulting alkyl radical undergoes ortho-(4+2) cyclization (giving cyclohexadienyl radical, then anodic oxidation/deprotonation to product), whereas the vinyl radical undergoes ipso-(3+2) spirocyclization followed by SET oxidation to a cation that is captured by MeO-/H2O to give the dienone.
rate_determining_step: SET oxidation/radical addition step: TS-1 (18.8 kcal/mol) for alkene pathway and TS-4 (17.1 kcal/mol) for alkyne pathway are the highest barriers; cyclization regiochemistry controlled by TS-3 vs TS-2 (alkyl, ortho favored by 2.0 kcal/mol) and TS-5 vs TS-6 (vinyl, ipso favored by 5.7 kcal/mol)

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely suppressed with 2.0 equiv, supporting radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: 2-methylthiophene
probe_reagents.observation: Captured the malonyl radical giving 5-thiophenyl adduct in 39% yield, confirming carbon radical

probe_reagents.name: H2-18O
probe_reagents.observation: 18O exclusively incorporated into carbonyl of spiro product 22 (18O:16O > 20:1), indicating carbonyl O comes from water, not methoxy/MeOH

probe_reagents.name: Cp2FePF6 (stoichiometric)
probe_reagents.observation: As stoichiometric chemical oxidant, gave spiro 22 in 15% yield, supporting Cp2Fe+ as active oxidant

probe_reagents.name: no Cp2Fe control
probe_reagents.observation: No product formed without ferrocene mediator

probe_reagents.name: absence of alkene/alkyne
probe_reagents.observation: Homo-coupling dimer of malonate formed in 33% yield, consistent with malonyl radical

notes: Regiodivergence is substrate-controlled: alkyl (sp3) radical from alkene addition prefers ortho-(4+2) cyclization (interaction energy dominant, ΔΔEint = -10.2), while vinyl (sp2) radical from alkyne addition prefers ipso-(3+2) spirocyclization (distortion energy dominant). 7-Cl-dimethoxy intermediate (product 28) was isolated rather than the oxo-product, providing evidence for the cation VI / dimethoxy VII pathway prior to hydrolysis to dienone. Cp2Fe acts as outer-sphere SET mediator, confirmed by CV showing catalytic current only with Cp2Fe + 1a + NaOMe.
